meta.title: OpenCairn — AI Knowledge OS
meta.description: Turn files and notes into a grounded wiki with AI workflows, evidence, and connection surfaces. Self-hostable · AGPLv3 + commercial license.
meta.ogDescription: OpenCairn weaves PDFs, papers, and documents into a wiki with grounded Q&A and graph surfaces.

hero.label: HERO
hero.labelMeta: 2026.04 · SEOUL · AI KNOWLEDGE OS · DUAL LICENSE
hero.sub: Drop in PDFs, papers, documents, and supported media. AI weaves them into a wiki, suggests connections, and checks how well you understand what's there. The AI workflow backbone activates in stages.

activity.title: PREVIEW · WORKSPACE
activity.footer: Agent activity demo (real runs ship in stages)

items.text: 4 weak concepts detected · review cards suggested
4 new question candidates indexed · Q&A ready
Attention score heatmap · rendered in browser

pipeline.label: PIPELINE
pipeline.sub: Instead of one giant prompt, narrow-responsibility steps parse, embed, draft, link, and serve the knowledge base. Answers and graph surfaces carry source chips and evidence panels.

steps.title: Intake
steps.body: PDF · DOCX · PPTX · XLSX · HWP/HWPX · Markdown/CSV ZIP · Google Drive. OCR and media handling depend on provider capability and deployment settings.

steps.title: Break down
steps.body: Text, table structure, and OCR output are normalized to Markdown. Media transcription runs through configured provider paths.

steps.title: Meaning
steps.body: Mapped to vector space. Similar things end up close.

steps.title: Weave
steps.body: The compile workflow drafts wiki pages per topic.

steps.title: Link
steps.body: Backlinks · opposing views · causal edges into a knowledge graph.

steps.title: Use
steps.body: Q&A · review cards · Socratic tutor · document-generation surfaces.

citation.label: CITATION
citation.title: Answers and graph views trace back to source evidence.
citation.body: AI answers carry source chips, while graph, card, and mindmap surfaces expose evidence panels. Sentence-level runtime verification is not yet enforced across every writer path.

agents.label: AGENTS
agents.sub: Twelve roles are defined in the long-term architecture. The shipped product mixes runtime agents, workflow-backed features, and feature-flagged surfaces — check the public roadmap for what's currently active.

items.name: Compiler
items.body: Extracts concepts from uploaded sources into wiki pages. Supported files and provider paths depend on configuration.

items.name: Research
items.body: Hybrid Q&A across multiple search strategies, with source chips on answers.

items.name: Librarian
items.body: Finds orphan pages, contradictions, and duplicates. Wiki health on a schedule.

items.name: Synthesis
items.body: Finds structural analogies across domains — immune system ↔ resilience.

items.name: Connector
items.body: Suggests cross-project links; accept to create them.

items.name: Temporal
items.body: Looks for stale knowledge and review candidates. The active scope depends on deployment configuration.

items.name: Curator
items.body: Suggests external-source candidates for gaps in the wiki. Search paths depend on provider and configuration.

items.name: Deep Research
items.body: A longer-running workflow for follow-up topic candidates and report drafts, enabled in staged phases.

items.name: Socratic
items.body: Checks understanding through dialogue and suggests review-card candidates. Spaced repetition continues in the learning surface.

items.name: Narrator
items.body: Long-term role for turning wiki content into review scripts and audio outputs.

items.name: Code
items.body: Handles code workspaces, approved runs, and static previews in staged phases. Server execution only runs behind feature flags and sandbox boundaries.

items.name: Visualization
items.body: Renders the graph as Graph · Mindmap · Cards · Timeline · Board.

views.label: VIEWS
views.title: One graph, five lenses.
views.sub: One dataset, five ways to see it. Switch to whichever view fits the moment. Every switch is logged and reversible.

items.name: Graph
items.body: Explore the full network at a glance

items.name: Mindmap
items.body: Radiate outward from a central concept

items.name: Cards
items.body: Search, filter, and spaced-repetition review

items.name: Timeline
items.body: Changes across time

items.name: Board
items.body: Collect related cards in one working board

try.label: TRY IT
try.sub: A sample graph that shows how transformer papers can be woven into a wiki. Hover or tap a node to see how connections spread.
bullets: Each node is an example wiki page.
Edges are relationships found via embeddings and shared concepts.
The numbers show how often other pages reference this one.
try.caption: Knowledge graph · 8 nodes · 9 edges

attention.d: A mechanism that weighs different parts of the input sequence so the model can focus on what matters.

transformer.d: A sequence-to-sequence architecture built from attention alone. Encoder–decoder structure.

multihead.d: Runs attention in parallel subspaces to capture different kinds of relationships.

positional.d: Attention has no innate notion of order — position must be injected explicitly.

encoder.d: Stack that turns input tokens into representation vectors. The upper half of a Transformer.

decoder.d: Uses encoder output to generate the target sequence autoregressively.

bert.d: Encoder-only bidirectional pretrained model. Strong on understanding tasks.

rope.d: Position info via rotation matrices. Better extrapolation than naive positional encodings.

pretraining.d: Learn general language ability on a large corpus, then transfer to downstream tasks.

workspace.label: WORKSPACE
workspace.title1: Same shape for a solo user
workspace.sub: Wiki pages aren't just text — they're the meeting point of sources, agents, and teammates. Every page carries its citations, editors, and agent activity with it.

mock.body: Attention weights the tokens of an input sequence by relevance. Vaswani et al. (2017) — "Attention Is All You Need" — introduced it as the foundation of the transformer architecture; self-attention and multi-head attention followed.
mock.calloutBody: Three sources disagree on quadratic complexity. The Linear Attention family challenges this assumption.

feed.text: contradiction detected in source 4 vs source 9

principles.label: PRINCIPLES
principles.sub: Data sovereignty · living knowledge · one throughline to learning · open source. Tools that keep one or two of these are common. Keeping all four at once — that's OpenCairn.

items.title: Your data stays on your server.
items.body: Text, vectors, and original files all live in your infrastructure. With an Ollama-based local LLM configuration, model calls do not leave your deployment. It can also be run without external connectors.

items.title: AI does the sorting, you do the judging.
items.body: The Compiler agent extracts concepts, creates pages, and links them to the existing wiki. You review and edit. The time you spent copying by hand disappears.

items.title: Capture → explore → learn, one continuous line.
items.body: Q&A · graph surfaces · review cards · Socratic dialogue · document-generation surfaces — all fed from the same wiki. No tool-hopping.

items.title: Dual-licensed; exit always available.
items.body: Default is AGPLv3 — fork, modify, deploy yourself whenever you want. A separate commercial license is available for organizations that can't take an AGPL component. No risk of the service disappearing.

personas.label: WHO
personas.sub: Linearly scales from an individual to a lab to a regulated enterprise — without swapping tools.

items.title: Grad student · researcher
items.body: Papers and documents become one woven wiki. Explore relationships in the concept graph, ask grounded questions, and self-check before exams with AI quizzes. Citations lead back to your original notes.

items.title: Lab · small team
items.body: Run it with role-based permissions and live collaboration. Keep meeting notes and research sources in one project wiki, so knowledge stays behind when people move on.

items.title: Regulated industries
items.body: Designed with finance, healthcare, government, and universities in mind — places where external SaaS can be restricted. Fully-local LLM paths and commercial licensing can support stricter deployment policies.
bullets: · Audit and security requirements by design

install.label: INSTALL
install.sub: Docker Compose brings up the database, file storage, and workflow engine. After installing dependencies and running migrations, start the app processes; a profile flag enables fully-local Ollama mode.
bullets: Minimum 4 vCPU · 8GB RAM, recommended 8 vCPU · 16GB RAM
x86_64 + ARM64 multi-arch (Raspberry Pi · Apple Silicon · Oracle Ampere)
Backup and restore strategy documented; automation scripts are follow-up work

pricing.label: PRICING
pricing.sub: Open-source self-hosting is usable today. Managed cloud pricing, credits, refunds, and auto top-up will be finalized after the hosted billing rail and business registration.

solo.name: Solo
solo.tagline: Run it yourself · no app billing
bullets: AI workflows activate by flag and configuration
Data limits are controlled by your deployment settings
Bring your own Gemini key (BYOK) optional

secondary.label: OTHER ROUTES
secondary.title: Run it yourself, or on-prem.
secondary.sub: If managed cloud isn't for you, here are two more paths.

selfHost.title: Run it yourself with Docker
selfHost.body: Full open-source code (AGPLv3, with separate commercial license available) · self-operated workspaces and members · fully-local LLM option. Hardware and ops are on you.

enterprise.title: On-prem · auth · audit path
enterprise.body: Dedicated install and support path for finance, healthcare, government, and universities — environments where external SaaS can be restricted. Fully-local LLM configuration · organization auth integration design · commercial license inquiry.

items.q: Can OpenCairn analyze audio or video?
items.a: Yes when the supported media path is configured for your deployment. Audio and video are transcribed through provider and worker settings, then handled like text sources. Real-time transcription is not supported; files are processed after upload.

All of it in infrastructure you operate. Text, vectors, and wiki pages live in the database; original files live in file storage. In Gemini mode, AI calls go to the external provider. With an Ollama-based local LLM configuration, model calls do not leave your deployment. External connectors such as Google Drive or Notion only contact those services when enabled.

items.q: Doesn't AGPLv3 mean my company can't use it?
items.a: OpenCairn is dual-licensed. The default AGPLv3 can be evaluated for internal use, but network service models and source-disclosure obligations should go through your legal review. A separate commercial license is available for organizations whose policy disallows AGPL components or source disclosure.

The default paths are Gemini and Ollama, and OpenAI-compatible gateways can also be configured. Feature coverage varies by provider; Gemini-specific capabilities only work when that provider path is enabled.

items.q: Can I migrate from my existing note app?
items.a: Upload a Markdown/CSV export ZIP and Compiler re-weaves it into a wiki. Google Drive currently supports file-ID import. App-connected imports, including Notion, belong in the connector roadmap.

items.q: What if the AI gets the wiki wrong?
items.a: AI-generated changes go through review-and-apply flows, and you can edit them afterward. Note changes are tracked through versions and activity records; Librarian-style workflows are expanding toward duplicate and contradiction review.

items.q: How smooth is the real-time collaboration?
items.a: OpenCairn uses Hocuspocus and Yjs for synchronization. Collaboration surfaces such as cursors, comments, @mentions, notifications, and public links are implemented; real-world smoothness still depends on network and Hocuspocus deployment settings.

cta.label: CLOSING
cta.sub: Start free on Solo today. How much your knowledge base has grown a year from now depends on what you start accumulating today.
cta.bottomTonight: Tonight, start with your first PDF.

footer.tagline: AI knowledge OS. Solo or team. Self-hosted, forever.
